title: thelypteris squamatella
intro: Thelypteris squamatella, also known as the lizard fern, is a species of fern native to the subtropical regions of the Americas. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its rich content of tannins and flavonoids. The health benefits associated with Thelypteris squamatella include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe and protect the skin, as well as reduce the severity of joint pain. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and topical applications, such as salves and poultices, to harness its medicinal properties.
